news.title: Apollo Global commits $1 billion for retail joint venture with Realty Income
news.para: Realty Income is teaming up with Apollo Global Management to launch a new initiative. They're set to invest $1 billion into acquiring a 49% share of a portfolio filled with high-performing retail venues known for their consistent cash flow.

news.title: Shubham Housing  Finance raises $96 million from LeapFrog Investments, others
news.para: Shubham Housing Development Finance has secured USD 96 million in funding. Existing investor LeapFrog Investments and Creador are among the participants. This investment supports Shubham's expansion and strengthens its shareholder base. The company provides affordable housing finance to India's emerging middle class.

news.title: JSW Cement commissions Nagaur plant, enters north India market; capacity rises to 24.1 MTPA
news.para: JSW Cement has launched its new integrated cement plant in Nagaur, Rajasthan, marking its entry into the North India market. This facility, with a clinkerisation capacity of 3.3 MTPA and cement grinding capacity of 2.5 MTPA, boosts the company's total grinding capacity to 24.1 MTPA. The plant will serve Rajasthan, Haryana, Punjab, and the NCR, with further expansion planned.

news.title: Now, PM Gati Shakti to monitor Gujarat RERA projects
news.para: Gujarat's real estate and urban development departments are integrating GujRERA-registered projects with town planning data, utilizing the PM Gati Shakti platform for enhanced service delivery. This integration aims to spatially align projects with infrastructure, improve visibility of housing clusters, and support evidence-based urban governance for faster approvals and optimized investments.

news.title: Delhi government orders mapping, fire safety audit of commercial units citywide
news.para: Following a tragic fire in Palam that claimed nine lives, Delhi Home Minister Ashish Sood has ordered a comprehensive third-party fire safety audit of all city establishments. This initiative aims to identify safety gaps and ensure compliance with norms. The audit will cover commercial units in mixed-use areas.

news.title: Australia's Rest to invest up to $250 million in US retail property fund
news.para: Australian pension fund Rest is investing up to $250 million in a U.S. retail property fund. This move banks on traditional American retailing remaining strong. The investment targets neighbourhood shopping centres anchored by major grocery stores and retailers. Rest believes these assets offer stable returns and diversification.

news.title: JK Cement wins two limestone blocks, raises Muddapur grinding capacity to 4.5 MnTPA
news.para: JK Cement has secured two crucial limestone mining blocks in Madhya Pradesh and Andhra Pradesh, bolstering its raw material reserves. Simultaneously, the company has boosted its cement grinding capacity in Karnataka by one million tonnes per annum through operational enhancements. This strategic expansion elevates JK Cement's total grey cement production capacity to over 32 million tonnes annually.

news.title: Germany's Vonovia reports 4.2 billion euros profit in 2025
news.para: Germany's largest real estate group, Vonovia, has reported a return to profit in 2025. This marks a significant recovery after three consecutive years of losses. The company's financial turnaround was driven by an increase in property values. Vonovia plans to further reduce its debt by selling assets worth approximately 5 billion euros.

news.title: Maharashtra orders inquiry into ₹1,000 crore scam in BMC housing project
news.para: An irregularity in a BMC housing project for project-affected persons in Malad East has been admitted. Allegations include illegal land classification changes and inflated project costs. The developer received significant payments through credit notes and TDR without project commencement dates. Demands for a high-level inquiry are being made. The project faces delays of 10-20 years.

news.title: Homeland Group, VRC acquire Mohali land parcels for over ₹1,000 crore in GMADA auction
news.para: Homeland Group and VRC have jointly acquired two prime mixed-use land parcels in Mohali for over ₹1,000 crore. The acquisitions include a 5.5-acre parcel near the Chandigarh border for approximately ₹400 crore and a 13-acre site in Sector 62 for over ₹600 crore. Both developments will feature residential and commercial components, with completion targeted by early 2031.

news.title: Ahmedabad: 20 houses, four shops demolished to widen road in Narol
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation's estate department cleared land in Narol. Twenty residential houses and four shops were demolished. This action opened an 18-meter-wide road. Platforms and other encroachments were also removed. The corporation had issued prior notices to occupants. The demolition occurred in the presence of police after non-compliance.

news.title: Vadodara: Man cheats 16 of ₹40 lakh in PMAY flat scam
news.para: Vadodara police have registered a cheating case against Harish Chauhan, who allegedly defrauded 16 individuals by promising PMAY flats.  Chauhan, posing as a VMC agent, collected approximately Rs 40 lakh from victims before disappearing.  One complainant reported paying Rs 6.83 lakh for a promised flat in Subhanpura. The accused is currently absconding.

news.title: Telangana HC to hear plea against demolition of houses on Bhoodan land
news.para: The Telangana High Court will hear a petition on March 23 challenging the demolition of houses allegedly built on Bhoodan land in Khammam district. Twenty daily wage workers claim their homes were razed by authorities on February 24 without prior notice, despite having received pattas for the plots. They seek suspension of demolition orders and compensation.

news.title: Karnataka eyes Japan partnership for smart city solutions in Bengaluru
news.para: Karnataka is set to collaborate with Japan on smart city initiatives in Bengaluru, aiming to leverage Japanese expertise for urban challenges. This partnership, facilitated by Japan's MLIT, will focus on areas like energy, wastewater, mobility, and disaster management, with the Greater Bengaluru Authority acting as the nodal agency.

news.title: UltraTech Cement to acquire 26.18% stake in Sunsure Solarpark for ₹19.2 crore
news.para: UltraTech Cement is going green by investing up to ₹19.2 crore in a stake of Sunsure Solarpark Seven. With this initiative, the company aims to tap into renewable energy, curbing its electricity expenses and fulfilling environmental regulations. The deal, which will be finalized with a cash infusion, is projected to be completed in 180 days.

news.title: Second phase of Indiramma housing scheme in Telangana to be launched in April
news.para: The second phase of the Indiramma housing program is set to launch in April and May, with the government aiming to construct houses in every urban ward over the next three years. A significant number of houses from the first phase are expected to be completed by June or July, with a special focus on urban areas and tribal regions.

news.title: Illegal housing units in Gurugram's Palam Vihar face sealing, water & power connections to be snapped
news.para: Authorities in Gurugram are sealing numerous illegally constructed dwelling units in Palam Vihar following a Punjab and Haryana High Court directive. The crackdown includes disconnecting essential utilities for these properties, which were found to exceed permissible construction norms and strain local infrastructure. This action aims to enforce planned development and address violations previously deemed insufficient.

news.title: Ghaziabad development body collects only 50% of revenue target
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority (GDA) has fallen short of its revenue targets for compounding fees in 2025-26, collecting only 50% of the projected amount. The authority also ranked among the top five underperforming districts in Uttar Pradesh for mutation policy implementation in February. This poor performance raises concerns about GDA's operational efficiency and its impact on overall earnings.
